source: Learning Ansible with Rocky - Management Server Optimizations: Windows, network modules, and generating a CMDB

questions.q: To manage Windows servers, which python package provides WinRM support?
questions.why: pip3 install pywinrm (plus kerberos packages) enables WinRM management.

questions.q: Which packages/config support Kerberos authentication to Windows realms?
questions.why: Install krb5-devel/libs/workstation and configure realms in /etc/krb5.conf.

questions.q: Which python module do the network/IP modules usually require?
questions.why: sudo pip3 install netaddr is needed for the IP/network modules.

questions.q: What does the ansible-cmdb tool generate?
options: A CMDB (configuration database) built from Ansible facts
questions.why: ansible-cmdb turns exported setup facts into a CMDB.

questions.q: How are the facts exported for ansible-cmdb?
options: With the setup module and --tree, e.g. ansible -m setup --tree <dir>
questions.why: ansible -m setup --tree <dir> writes per-host facts that ansible-cmdb then reads.

questions.q: ansible-cmdb can output which formats (per the examples)?
options: json, and an html web interface (html_fancy_split)
questions.why: ansible-cmdb -t json or -t html_fancy_split produce a JSON file or an HTML interface.
